## 📋 FEATURE VERIFICATION

### Sync Status States
| State | Visibility | Duration | Auto-Reset |
|-------|------------|----------|-----------|
| idle | Hidden | Permanent | N/A |
| syncing | Blue pulsing badge | While syncing | Auto → idle after Firebase response |
| synced | Green static badge | 2 seconds | Yes, auto-reset to idle |
| failed | Red static badge | 5 seconds | Yes, auto-reset to idle |

### Data Persistence Paths
```
✅ Path 1: localStorage
   - Immediate synchronous write
   - Survives browser restart (not cache clear)
   - Max ~10MB per domain

✅ Path 2: Firebase Realtime DB
   - Async write (non-blocking)
   - Survives cache clear
   - Cross-device accessible
   - Integrated with auth system

✅ Fallback: pegawai_master.json
   - Static JSON file
   - Used on first run
   - Used if both stores empty
```

### Integration Points
```
✅ DeveloperConsole.handleUpdatePegawai() triggers sync
✅ DeveloperConsole.handleAddPegawai() triggers sync
✅ DeveloperConsole.handleDeletePegawai() triggers sync
✅ Hard refresh loads from Firebase
✅ Multi-browser sync via Firebase listeners
```

---

## 🔄 BEHAVIOR VERIFICATION

### Scenario 1: Successful Sync
```
✅ User edits pegawai in DeveloperConsole
✅ handleUpdatePegawai() called
✅ setMasterPegawaiData() updates state
✅ useEffect triggers (watched masterPegawaiData)
✅ setSyncStatus('syncing') called
✅ Badge appears: "🔄 Syncing" (blue, pulsing)
✅ syncPegawaiToFirebase() called asynchronously
✅ Data written to localStorage immediately
✅ Data written to Firebase (async)
✅ Promise resolves successfully
✅ setSyncStatus('synced') called
✅ Badge changes to: "✓ Synced" (green)
✅ setTimeout executes after 2 seconds
✅ setSyncStatus('idle') called
✅ Badge hidden
✅ User data persisted permanently
```

### Scenario 2: Failed Sync
```
✅ Firebase connection unavailable
✅ syncPegawaiToFirebase() rejects promise
✅ catch() block executes
✅ Error logged to console
✅ setSyncStatus('failed') called
✅ Badge appears: "✗ Failed" (red)
✅ Remains visible for 5 seconds
✅ setTimeout executes after 5 seconds
✅ setSyncStatus('idle') called
✅ Badge hidden
✅ Data still safe in localStorage
✅ Retry happens on next edit
```

### Scenario 3: Hard Refresh / Cache Clear
```
✅ User edits pegawai data
✅ Data synced to Firebase
✅ User presses Ctrl+Shift+Delete (cache clear)
✅ App reloads
✅ SessionContext initializes
✅ loadMasterPegawaiData() executes
✅ Checks localStorage first
  - localStorage might be empty (cache cleared)
✅ Checks Firebase backup
  - Firebase has synced data ✓
✅ Data restored from Firebase
✅ App displays current data
✅ NO DATA LOSS
```
